description: You are a Quantitative Reliability Engineer building a 'Live Z-Score' dashboard for web traffic to prevent alert fatigue from static thresholds. Create and save a Graphite dashboard named 'Statistical Traffic Z-Score' containing three graphs. The 'Traffic Baseline' graph must display the raw metric `servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1` and its 1-hour moving average `movingAverage(servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1, '1h')`. The 'Traffic Volatility' graph must display the 1-hour rolling standard deviation `movingStdDev(servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1, '1h')`. The 'Live Z-Score' graph must compute the Z-score (Z = (X - μ) / σ) using Graphite functions to compare the raw metric against its 1-hour moving average and standard deviation. This graph must also include reference lines titled '+3 Sigma' and '-3 Sigma' at values 3 and -3 using the threshold function.
